steps.title: Spot the contradiction
steps.content: The first sentence states an assumption: offshore and land farms produce similar energy. The second sentence immediately refutes this, explaining that offshore turbines actually produce more energy. A transition word must signal this shift from assumption to correction.

steps.title: Choose the correction word
steps.content: ”Actually” is a correction word—it marks when reality contradicts an initial assumption. The other three don't signal contradiction: “For example” would support the previous idea, “Therefore” would introduce a logical consequence, and “Finally” would mark a concluding point in a sequence. That matches choice A — A is correct.
